analysis.problem: Housing segregation and potential loss of homes in urban areas
analysis.root_cause: Inadequate urban planning and housing policies, leading to segregation and unaffordability
leverage_points: Government policies and regulations on urban planning and housing
Real estate developers and their investment strategies

proposed_actions.action: Implement inclusive zoning policies to promote mixed-income housing
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase the number of affordable housing units by 20% within the next 2 years

proposed_actions.action: Provide incentives for real estate developers to build affordable housing
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase the number of affordable housing units by 30% within the next 5 years

evidence: New residential developments in the UK are often segregating people based on those who own their homes and those that live in social housing
We can’t build our way out of the housing crisis while losing homes every day
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of civilization, human dignity, and biosphere, as it affects the well-being and quality of life of urban residents and the sustainability of urban ecosystems
analysis.summary: Housing segregation and potential loss of homes in urban areas is a significant problem that requires immediate attention and action from policymakers and real estate developers. Inclusive zoning policies and incentives for affordable housing can help address this issue.
